mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, labor market deterioration signals imminent recessionary pressure, causing equity risk premiums to spike and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 9.0% threshold, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer price inflation creates stagflationary expectations, forcing monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually alongside rising RSAFS, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, monthly increases in the industrial production index reflect rapid capacity utilization and earnings revisions, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over five trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls indicate robust consumer spending capacity and corporate revenue growth, consistently driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll growth above 150000 monthly fails to correlate with positive forward returns, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly inflation readings breach 0.4%, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening, increasing discount rates and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% threshold, or the signal loses predictive power during disinflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in housing starts and real GDP growth signals synchronized economic strength across residential and broader sectors, producing positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to generate positive returns when HOUST growth decouples from GDPC1, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, declining industrial production signals weakening manufacturing demand and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal produces positive returns in more than 50% of observed instances.
